sanskrit: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत । तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
hindiTranslation: हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन सबके देहमें यह देही नित्य ही अवध्य है ।  इसलिये सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके लिये अर्थात् किसी भी प्राणीके लिये तुम्हें शोक नहीं करना चाहिये ॥
englishTranslation: O descendant of Bharata, the embodied one in every body is eternal and cannot be slain. Therefore you should not grieve for any being.
essence: What is most real in a person cannot be killed, so grief loses its ground.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The true self in every body cannot be destroyed, so there is no reason to grieve for anyone.
simpleInsight: Loss feels different when you remember that what is deepest in a person cannot be cut off.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is frozen by grief and fear. Krishna answers by pointing to the deepest reality: the embodied one in every body is not destroyed. That truth undercuts Arjuna’s sorrow and prepares him for duty.
modernRelevance: You stand beside a hospital bed, hearing a diagnosis you cannot fix. Panic says everything is ending; this verse says what is deepest in a person is beyond harm.
translationLiteral: देही (the embodied one) / नित्यम् (always, eternally) / अवध्यः (not to be slain) / अयम् (this) / देहे (in the body) / सर्वस्य (of all) / भारत (O Bharata) / तस्मात् (therefore) / सर्वाणि (all) / भूतानि (beings) / न (not) / त्वम् (you) / शोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (you should)
insight: It shifts the mind from mourning the body alone to seeing the enduring presence within it.

quoteVariants.text: The embodied one cannot be slain. Grief for any being is misplaced.
Nothing living is ever truly destroyed.
